Public lands belong to all of us. But right now, they’re being threatened by privatization, pollution, and the Trump administration’s drill-first agenda. Our nation's irreplaceable public lands and waters deserve better than reckless development. We need your help to defend them! Donate today >>

Congress hasn’t done a damn thing to stop the destruction of America’s public lands. So we wrote the bill ourselves. It’s called the POPULAR Act. It doesn’t cost a cent. It doesn’t create new agencies. It just restores the protections both parties supported for decades – before greed took over. We’re talking about the rules that kept drilling rigs, logging mandates, and bulldozers in check. No more mandatory drilling. No more forced logging quotas. No more loopholes that let corporations rubber-stamp their own reviews. No more silence while public land gets sold off piece by piece. The Democrats could make this law right now. They have the leverage in the fall budget fight. But they won’t use it unless we make them. So here it is. Read it. Send it to your representatives. Share it widely. This is the bill Congress should already have passed, especially before the next season of destruction begins.

We must protect the lands that embody the legacy, beauty, and history of our nation and its people. Doing so requires diligence and strategic action—conserving our natural treasures while promoting the sustainable use of resources. Our land deserves the same respect and pride that she instills in us as Americans. URGENT 🚨: Right now Republicans in the Senate are pushing the largest public lands sell-off in history. Nearly 3 million acres are at risk of being liquidated — paving the way for mega-mansions, gated communities, mining, logging, and oil drilling in some of the West's most spectacular places, all for the profit of a few. America's public lands are simply irreplaceable. They provide habitat for imperiled wildlife like grizzly bears and spotted owls, and are a crucial source of clean water, healthy air, and a respite for all who visit. Please urge your senators right now to oppose the sell-off of public lands. ➡️ biodiv.us/handsoffpubliclands

In the dense rainforests of Sumatra, one of the world’s rarest tigers still fights to survive. With poaching and deforestation closing in, your voice matters more than ever. 🐅 Like our Page to protect what's left - before it's too late.

🐒 Monkey business in El Salvador! At Puerto Barillas, we got to feed spider monkeys and watch them swing from tree to tree—definitely one of the highlights of our trip. On the walk through the sanctuary, we spotted tons of birds and even a baby crocodile in the swamp! 🐊 Spider monkeys are endangered, mostly due to deforestation and illegal pet trade. Ecotourism helps protect these incredible creatures and the mangrove ecosystem they call home. 🦧🌴 Indonesia’s Leuser Ecosystem is the ONLY place on Earth where orangutans, elephants, and tigers still roam together in the wild. But it’s being torn apart to make room for cheap palm oil. Help us raise $10,000 in June to protect the Leuser — and thanks to a matching gift, every dollar you give will be doubled!

A pair of baby siamang gibbons enjoy a grooming session after their mornings breakfast at the @sumatran_rescue_alliance sanctuary. Victims of the illegal wildlife trade they are 2 of 10 that were confiscated from a ship off the coast of North Sumatra, destined for Malaysia. After that it’s not as clear. Maybe to the Middle East where exotic pets are still popular. Or to India where a boom in wildlife trafficking is seeing a massive amount of exotic wildlife heading there. But why? The Middle East has been a well known wildlife trading hot spot for a long time. A lot of wealthy people are wanting to show off that wealth. Unfortunately exotic pets are a big draw card. Big cats and primates are highly sort after. But now gibbons are becoming a very popular option. Regarded as “small apes” they get far less attention their great ape cousins. In India it’s believed with the rapid increase in wealthy people in the country (almost 200 billionaires) there is more money available to spend on rare things and exotic pets are growing in popularity largely thanks to social media posts. There is also one of the world’s largest and newest zoos that’s opened. There is a loophole that illegal wildl …

SOMWHERE WILD... - Scientific name: Pongo pygmaeus - I've just spent 15 days in the heart of Borneos jungles, and while the whole experience was incredible, it really broadened my understanding. My single most favourite experience of the trip was captured in this image. I sat in awe as I watched this female orangutan use incredible brute strength, She husked a coconut with ease, cracked it on a tree, and then sipped with gentle elegance, all right in front of me A simple moment of raw beauty… yet behind it lies a heartbreaking reality. This size of palm oil plantation in comparison to jungle had me taken back. Seeing what I've only read about, now right in front of my eyes. It was a bittersweet experience. Orangutans are one of our closest relatives, sharing around 97% of our DNA, but as each day goes by, they are closer and closer to extinction. In the last 20 years, orangutans have lost over 80% of their habitat. Palm oil plantations bulldoze entire rainforests, leaving them homeless and hungry. Babies are stolen for the illegal pet trade, and in order to take the baby, an inseparable bond must be broken, and so their mothers are brutally killed in the process. During the fires o …
